messages.content:
You are an expert Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) tasked with diagnosing performance issues and failures in the Bookstore Testbed, a Spring Boot microservice environment.
You are operating with framework-native Spring Boot Actuator observability.

## Testbed Architecture
The environment consists of three microservices running on Kubernetes:
  - inventory-service: Manages the book catalog and inventory levels. Includes a database connection pool.
  - order-service: Orchestrates order placement and interacts with the inventory-service. Includes a database connection pool.
  - payment-service: Simulates payment processing. Does not have a database.

Note: Database connection pool metrics (HikariCP) are only available for inventory-service and order-service.

## Investigation Guidelines
- Begin by checking the health of all services to identify where degradation is occurring.
- Parallel tool calls are encouraged to speed up data collection, but focus your efforts once a degraded service is identified.
- Avoid redundant checks on services already confirmed healthy.
- A service reporting healthy or ready does not rule out underlying resource or runtime issues.
  Health probes reflect availability, not the internal state of resources such as CPU, memory, or thread usage.
  If other signals suggest a problem, continue investigating even if health reports UP.
- Metrics and resource readings represent point-in-time snapshots. A single reading is not
  sufficient to characterise resource behaviour. When a metric reading appears abnormal,
  collect repeated observations across separate tool calls before drawing conclusions about
  the nature or severity of the issue.
- Base your diagnosis on evidence that is consistent across multiple observations, not on
  a single data point. Transient spikes and gradual trends require different conclusions
  and both require more than one reading to distinguish.
- Once you have identified a root cause supported by consistent evidence across your
  investigation, use submit_diagnosis.
- If all services appear healthy after thorough inspection, submit a diagnosis with no_fault_detected=True.

## Diagnosis Submission Requirements
When calling submit_diagnosis, provide the following:
  - service, component, fault_type: Select the most accurate values from the provided enums.
  - evidence: A concise summary of the logs, metrics, or events that support your conclusion (min 80 characters).
    Evidence must reflect your full investigation, not a single isolated reading.
  - no_fault_detected: Set to True ONLY if all services are verified healthy. If True, set service, component, and fault_type to None.

Investigate the bookstore microservice testbed. Check the health and performance of all services. Identify any faults or anomalies if present. When you have completed your investigation, call submit_diagnosis with your findings. If all services are healthy, set no_fault_detected=True.
I'll start by checking the health of all three microservices to identify where degradation is occurring.

{"tool": "get_service_health_b", "status": "success", "data": {"status": "UP", "groups": ["liveness", "readiness"], "components": {"circuitBreakers": {"status": "UNKNOWN"}, "db": {"status": "UP", "details": {"database": "PostgreSQL", "validationQuery": "isValid()"}}, "diskSpace": {"status": "UP", "details": {"total": 1081101176832, "free": 1017830916096, "threshold": 10485760, "path": "/workspace/.", "exists": true}}, "livenessState": {"status": "UP"}, "ping": {"status": "UP"}, "readinessState": {"status": "UP"}, "ssl": {"status": "UP", "details": {"validChains": [], "invalidChains": []}}}}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:30:19Z", "error_message": null}

messages.content:
All three services report as UP with healthy components. However, I notice that inventory-service has circuitBreakers status as "UNKNOWN" which might indicate an issue. Let me investigate further by checking the circuit breaker states and also looking at metrics to identify any underlying resource issues.

Let me check the circuit breaker states for all services and also look at some key metrics:

{"tool": "get_circuit_breaker_state", "status": "success", "data": {"circuit_breakers": {}, "event_count_total": 0, "events_shown": 0, "events": [], "events_note": "Prioritizes STATE_TRANSITION and ERROR events. Most recent first."}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:30:54Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: get_circuit_breaker_state

messages.content: {"tool": "get_circuit_breaker_state", "status": "success", "data": {"circuit_breakers": {"inventory": {"failureRate": "0.0%", "slowCallRate": "0.0%", "failureRateThreshold": "50.0%", "slowCallRateThreshold": "100.0%", "bufferedCalls": 10, "failedCalls": 0, "slowCalls": 0, "slowFailedCalls": 0, "notPermittedCalls": 0, "state": "CLOSED"}, "payment": {"failureRate": "0.0%", "slowCallRate": "0.0%", "failureRateThreshold": "50.0%", "slowCallRateThreshold": "100.0%", "bufferedCalls": 10, "failedCalls": 0, "slowCalls": 0, "slowFailedCalls": 0, "notPermittedCalls": 0, "state": "CLOSED"}}, "event_count_total": 20, "events_shown": 5, "events": [{"circuitBreakerName": "inventory", "type": "SUCCESS", "creationTime": "2026-05-12T18:30:54.001023147Z[GMT]", "errorMessage": null, "durationInMs": 39, "stateTransition": null}, {"circuitBreakerName": "payment", "type": "SUCCESS", "creationTime": "2026-05-12T18:30:51.947549703Z[GMT]", "errorMessage": null, "durationInMs": 211, "stateTransition": null}, {"circuitBreakerName": "inventory", "type": "SUCCESS", "creationTime": "2026-05-12T18:30:51.734950147Z[GMT]", "errorMessage": null, "durationInMs": 14, "stateTransition": null}, {"circuitBreakerName": "payment", "type": "SUCCESS", "creationTime": "2026-05-12T18:30:49.695890877Z[GMT]", "errorMessage": null, "durationInMs": 218, "stateTransition": null}, {"circuitBreakerName": "inventory", "type": "SUCCESS", "creationTime": "2026-05-12T18:30:49.472970177Z[GMT]", "errorMessage": null, "durationInMs": 18, "stateTransition": null}], "events_note": "Prioritizes STATE_TRANSITION and ERROR events. Most recent first."}, "service": "order-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:30:54Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: get_circuit_breaker_state

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "hikaricp.connections.active", "description": "Active connections", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 4.0}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "pool", "values": ["InventoryHikariPool"]}]}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:30:54Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "hikaricp.connections.active", "description": "Active connections", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 1.0}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "pool", "values": ["OrderHikariPool"]}]}, "service": "order-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:30:54Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content:
The circuit breaker states look healthy (CLOSED with 0% failure rates). The HikariCP active connections are also low (4 for inventory, 1 for order), which suggests the connection pools are not saturated.

Let me check more metrics to get a better picture of resource usage - JVM memory, CPU usage, and thread states:

{"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "jvm.memory.used", "description": "The amount of used memory", "base_unit": "bytes", "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 17.82, "unit": "percent_of_jvm_heap_limit", "heap_bytes_used": 95667032, "jvm_heap_max_bytes": 536870912}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "id", "values": ["Survivor Space", "Eden Space", "Tenured Gen"]}]}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:31:33Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "jvm.memory.used", "description": "The amount of used memory", "base_unit": "bytes", "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 11.34, "unit": "percent_of_jvm_heap_limit", "heap_bytes_used": 60885048, "jvm_heap_max_bytes": 536870912}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "id", "values": ["Survivor Space", "Eden Space", "Tenured Gen"]}]}, "service": "order-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:31:33Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "process.cpu.usage", "description": "The \"recent cpu usage\" for the Java Virtual Machine process", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 88.83, "unit": "percent_of_one_cpu_core"}], "available_tags": []}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:31:33Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "process.cpu.usage", "description": "The \"recent cpu usage\" for the Java Virtual Machine process", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 18.72, "unit": "percent_of_one_cpu_core"}], "available_tags": []}, "service": "order-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:31:33Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: Interesting! I found a significant issue: **inventory-service is showing 88.83% CPU usage**, which is very high (nearly 90% of one CPU core). This could indicate CPU saturation. Let me check the thread states and also get repeated readings to confirm this is a consistent issue:

{"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "jvm.threads.states", "description": "The current number of threads", "base_unit": "threads", "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 33.0}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "state", "values": ["timed-waiting", "new", "runnable", "blocked", "waiting", "terminated"]}]}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:04Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "hikaricp.connections.pending", "description": "Pending threads", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 0.0}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "pool", "values": ["InventoryHikariPool"]}]}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:04Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content: {"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "hikaricp.connections.timeout", "description": "Connection timeout total count", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "COUNT", "value": 0.0}], "available_tags": [{"tag": "pool", "values": ["InventoryHikariPool"]}]}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:04Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content:
The thread states show 33 threads total, which is reasonable. The pending connections are 0 and timeout count is 0, so the connection pool is not experiencing starvation issues.

Let me get repeated CPU readings to confirm this is a consistent high CPU usage issue:

{"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "process.cpu.usage", "description": "The \"recent cpu usage\" for the Java Virtual Machine process", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 99.71, "unit": "percent_of_one_cpu_core"}], "available_tags": []}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:21Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content:
Now I have consistent evidence of high CPU usage. The first reading was 88.83% and the second reading is 99.71%, both indicating severe CPU saturation on inventory-service. This is a clear sign of **CPU saturation**.

Let me also check if there are any application logs that might give more context about what's causing this high CPU usage:

{"tool": "get_application_logs", "status": "success", "data": {"level_filter": "WARN", "pod_name": "inventory-service-8df9c549f-qjjlm", "pod_restarted": false, "restart_count": 0, "current_logs": [{"raw": "Calculated JVM Memory Configuration: -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10M -Xmx230648K -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=127751K -XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=240M -Xss1M (Total Memory: 850M, Thread Count: 250, Loaded Class Count: 20141, Headroom: 0%)", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Enabling Java Native Memory Tracking", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Adding 146 container CA certificates to JVM truststore", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Spring Cloud Bindings Enabled", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Picked up JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS: -Djava.security.properties=/layers/paketo-buildpacks_bellsoft-liberica/java-security-properties/java-security.properties -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10M -Xmx230648K -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=127751K -XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=240M -Xss1M -XX:+UnlockDiagnosticVMOptions -XX:NativeMemoryTracking=summary -XX:+PrintNMTStatistics -Dorg.springframework.cloud.bindings.boot.enable=true", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Picked up _JAVA_OPTIONS: -Xms512m -Xmx512m -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=1g", "level": "UNKNOWN"}], "current_truncated": false, "previous_logs": null, "previous_truncated": null}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:36Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: get_application_logs

messages.content: The logs don't show any specific errors or warnings about CPU issues. Let me also check for ERROR level logs to see if there are any runtime exceptions:

{"tool": "get_application_logs", "status": "success", "data": {"level_filter": "ERROR", "pod_name": "inventory-service-8df9c549f-qjjlm", "pod_restarted": false, "restart_count": 0, "current_logs": [{"raw": "Calculated JVM Memory Configuration: -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10M -Xmx230648K -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=127751K -XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=240M -Xss1M (Total Memory: 850M, Thread Count: 250, Loaded Class Count: 20141, Headroom: 0%)", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Enabling Java Native Memory Tracking", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Adding 146 container CA certificates to JVM truststore", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Spring Cloud Bindings Enabled", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Picked up JAVA_TOOL_OPTIONS: -Djava.security.properties=/layers/paketo-buildpacks_bellsoft-liberica/java-security-properties/java-security.properties -XX:+ExitOnOutOfMemoryError -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=10M -Xmx230648K -XX:MaxMetaspaceSize=127751K -XX:ReservedCodeCacheSize=240M -Xss1M -XX:+UnlockDiagnosticVMOptions -XX:NativeMemoryTracking=summary -XX:+PrintNMTStatistics -Dorg.springframework.cloud.bindings.boot.enable=true", "level": "UNKNOWN"}, {"raw": "Picked up _JAVA_OPTIONS: -Xms512m -Xmx512m -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=1g", "level": "UNKNOWN"}], "current_truncated": false, "previous_logs": null, "previous_truncated": null}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:32:52Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: get_application_logs

messages.content: No ERROR logs showing. Let me also check the order-service CPU to confirm it's not experiencing similar issues:

{"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "process.cpu.usage", "description": "The \"recent cpu usage\" for the Java Virtual Machine process", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 4.11, "unit": "percent_of_one_cpu_core"}], "available_tags": []}, "service": "order-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:33:07Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content:
Order-service CPU is only at 4.11%, which is very low and healthy. This confirms that the issue is isolated to inventory-service.

Let me also check payment-service CPU to complete the picture:

{"tool": "query_actuator_metrics", "status": "success", "data": {"metric_name": "process.cpu.usage", "description": "The \"recent cpu usage\" for the Java Virtual Machine process", "base_unit": null, "measurements": [{"statistic": "VALUE", "value": 8.43, "unit": "percent_of_one_cpu_core"}], "available_tags": []}, "service": "payment-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:33:19Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: query_actuator_metrics

messages.content:
Payment-service CPU is at 8.43%, which is also healthy. 

Now I have a clear picture:
- **inventory-service**: CPU usage is extremely high (88.83% and 99.71%) - this indicates **CPU saturation**
- **order-service**: CPU usage is low (4.11%) - healthy
- **payment-service**: CPU usage is moderate (8.43%) - healthy

The evidence shows that inventory-service is experiencing severe CPU saturation, which could be due to:
1. An infinite loop or inefficient algorithm
2. Heavy computation in a tight loop
3. Resource-intensive operations without proper optimization

Let me submit the diagnosis with this finding:

args.evidence: Investigation of all three microservices revealed that inventory-service is experiencing severe CPU saturation with readings of 88.83% and 99.71% CPU usage (percent_of_one_cpu_core). Order-service shows healthy CPU at 4.11% and payment-service at 8.43%. The HikariCP connection pools are functioning normally with low active connections (4 for inventory, 1 for order), no pending connections, and zero timeout counts. Circuit breakers are in CLOSED state with 0% failure rates. JVM memory usage is healthy (17.82% for inventory, 11.34% for order). No ERROR logs or application exceptions detected. The root cause is CPU saturation on inventory-service, likely due to an inefficient algorithm, infinite loop, or resource-intensive computation consuming excessive CPU resources.

messages.content: {"tool": "submit_diagnosis", "status": "success", "data": {"submitted": true, "no_fault_detected": false, "service": "inventory-service", "component": "cpu", "fault_type": "cpu-saturation", "evidence": "Investigation of all three microservices revealed that inventory-service is experiencing severe CPU saturation with readings of 88.83% and 99.71% CPU usage (percent_of_one_cpu_core). Order-service shows healthy CPU at 4.11% and payment-service at 8.43%. The HikariCP connection pools are functioning normally with low active connections (4 for inventory, 1 for order), no pending connections, and zero timeout counts. Circuit breakers are in CLOSED state with 0% failure rates. JVM memory usage is healthy (17.82% for inventory, 11.34% for order). No ERROR logs or application exceptions detected. The root cause is CPU saturation on inventory-service, likely due to an inefficient algorithm, infinite loop, or resource-intensive computation consuming excessive CPU resources."}, "service": "inventory-service", "timestamp_utc": "2026-05-12T18:34:02Z", "error_message": null}
messages.name: submit_diagnosis
